title: 8 Managing Application Access and Permissions
body_text:
8	Managing Application Access and Permissions
8.1	Overview of Managing Application Access and Permissions
User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) can assign, modify, and remove application access and permissions for Client Centre Users and other Security Officers. Security Officers must have administrative rights to an application to assign it to another user in User Management. See Section 4.4, Assigning Administrative Rights to a User Management Security Officer, for more information.
Some applications only require a Security Officer to assign access, while other applications also have permissions to assign (e.g., user type, transaction limits, transit). See Section 8.2, Managing Application Access and Permissions, for a general description of those workflows. See the appropriate section in this chapter for procedures related to a specific application.
Some applications require approval from another Security Officer. See the appropriate section for application-specific approval requirements. See Chapter 9, Managing Requests, for procedures on approving requests.
This chapter applies to both Client Centre Users and managing personal access rights to applications for Security Officers.
8.2	Managing Application Access and Permissions
This section provides general procedures on assigning and removing access to application, editing application permissions, and removing a transit number. However, as some applications require specific permissions while others donГўВҖВҷt, application-specific information is provided in the proceeding sections. Some applications also have a separate user management component, i.e., users may require set-up in both the User Management application and the individual application.
Note:	When assigning personal access rights to a Security Officer, ensure that you select
Add
in the ГўВҖВңAccessГўВҖВқ column next to the appropriate application. See Figure 1. Selecting
Add
in the ГўВҖВңAdminГўВҖВқ column gives the Security Officer administrative rights to the application. See Section 4.4, Assigning Administrative Rights to a User Management Security Officer, for procedures.
Figure 1: Add Personal Access Rights to a User Management Security Officer
Assign, modify, or remove applications and edit permissions as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Locate the appropriate application in the Applications section. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: The Applications Section
Assigning an Application
Assign an application as follows:
Select
Add
next to the appropriate application. See Figure 2 above.
If there arenГўВҖВҷt any permissions associated with the application, select
OK
to confirm.
If there are permissions associated with the application, complete the additional application fields as described in the proceeding sections.
Have another Security Officer authorize the change if required.
Note:	See the appropriate section for application-specific approval requirements.
Removing an Application
To remove an application:
Select
Remove
next to the applicable application. See Figure 2 above.
Select
OK
to confirm.
Editing Application Permissions
To edit the permissions associated with an application:
Select
Edit
next to the applicable application. See Figure 2 above.
Note:	The Edit option only displays next to an application if the user has access to the application, and the application has permissions that can be edited.
Update the application-specific permissions as described in the proceeding sections.
Note: 	The language in the application-specific sections focuses on adding application access and permissions. When editing application access, deselect permissions or otherwise update fields as required. The necessary actions will be intuitive if following the procedures in the following sections.
Have another Security Officer authorize the change if required. See the appropriate section for application-specific approval requirements.
Removing a Transit Number
Some applications have a field that associates the user to a specific transit number(s), e.g., Branch Capture, Corporate Capture,  Money Transfer System (MTS), and the Online Tracing System (OLT). To remove a transit number from an application:
Select
Edit
next to the applicable application. See Figure 2 above.
Select
remove
next to the transit number, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Removing a Transit
Have another Security Officer authorize the change if required. See the appropriate section for application-specific approval requirements.
8.3	Branch Capture
Branch Capture deposit processing allows authorized users at your financial institution to scan images of paper deposit items and submit them to Central 1 electronically.
Branch Capture has four user types that you can assign to users: Capture User, Reviewer, Supervisor, and Administrator. Individuals can be set up with one user type or multiple user types. A userГўВҖВҷs tasks, experience, and responsibilities differ based on how user types are assigned. Your financial institution decides how to set up users. See the Deposit Processing Part for Branch Capture workflows and user type descriptions.
Set up a Branch Capture User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңBranch CaptureГўВҖВқ. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Add Branch Capture
Complete the Branch Capture permissions as follows. See Figure 5.
ГўВ‘
Add Transit Number(s)
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the first few digits of the transit number to which the user needs access, then select the appropriate transit number from the list. Alternatively, enter an unlisted transit number.
ГўВҲВ’
To add multiple transit numbers to a user (if applicable), enter a transit number, then select the
Enter
key on your keyboard to create a new line. Repeat until youГўВҖВҷve entered all transit numbers.  You can assign a maximum of 44 transits.
Note:	Enter your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs head office transit number if the user is a Branch Capture Administrator.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Add
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select the User Types(s)
ГўВҖВ“ Select the checkbox(es) next to the user type(s) to which the user requires access. You can assign multiple user types to a user. Choose from:
ГўВҲВ’
CU Administration Manager
ГўВҖВ“ Views deposits and creates reports for all financial institution transits (including Corporate Capture deposits).
ГўВҲВ’
CU Branch Supervisor
ГўВҖВ“ Reviews and transmits deposits. Creates deposit reports for all users at assigned transit(s).
ГўВҲВ’
CU Branch Deposit Clerk
ГўВҖВ“ Review
ГўВҖВ“ Reviews and approves deposits. Generates deposit reports for all users at assigned transit(s).
ГўВҲВ’
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Capture
ГўВҖВ“ Creates and balances deposits. Generates deposit reports for owned deposits.
Select
Submit
.
Figure 5: Complete Branch Capture Permissions
Review the summary of changes, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Note:	This application doesnГўВҖВҷt require approval from another Security Officer.
8.4	Business Taxes
Business Taxes allows authorized users at your financial institution to submit business tax payments and filings electronically to the CRA and to view filing and remittance history via
PaymentStream
Direct. Business Taxes isnГўВҖВҷt connected to your banking system. See the CRA Business Taxes Part for more information.
Set up a Business Taxes User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңPaymentStream Direct Business TaxesГўВҖВқ. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Add PaymentStream Direct Business Taxes
Select
OK
to confirm.
Note:	This application doesnГўВҖВҷt require approval from another Security Officer.
8.5	Campaign Manager
Campaign Manager allows authorized users at your financial institution to manage online banking marketing campaigns. Campaign Manager manages the delivery of Enhanced Personal Messages (EPM) and Intercept Marketing messages. An EPM is a message that is sent to a specified list of customers via online banking within a scheduled timeframe. Customers view EPMs in their online banking message summary. An Intercept Marketing message is a message that displays to a specified list of online banking customers prior to viewing the Account Summary page. See the Campaign Manager User Guide for more information.
Set up a Campaign Manager User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңPaymentStream Direct Campaign ManagerГўВҖВқ. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Add Campaign Manager
Complete the
PaymentStream
Direct Campaign Manager permissions as follows. See FigureВ 8.
ГўВҲВ’
Select the checkbox(es) next to the appropriate Campaign Manager permission(s):
ГўВҲВ’
EPM Manager
ГўВҖВ“
View, create, publish, and rollback EPM Campaigns.
ГўВҲВ’
Intercept Manager
ГўВҖВ“ View, create, publish, and rollback Intercept Marketing Campaigns.
Select
Submit
.
Figure 8: Select Campaign Manager Permissions.
Review the summary of changes, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Related Information
Campaign Manager User Guide
8.6	Cash Services
Cash Services allows authorized users at your financial institution to order Canadian and US bank notes and Canadian coin from Royal Bank.
There are two user management components for Cash Services users. First, users must be set up as a Client Centre User or Security Officer with access to Cash Services. Second, Cash Services Admin Users or Access Management Users create profiles for other users and assign permissions in the Cash Services application. This section describes how to assign access to Cash Services via User Management. See the Currency Ordering Part for procedures on managing users in the Cash Services application.
Set up a Cash Services User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңCash ServicesГўВҖВқ. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Add Cash Services
Select
OK
to confirm.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
8.7	Commercial Real Estate Lending
Commercial Real Estate Lending allows authorized users at your financial institutions to access documents related to the Central 1 Commercial loan syndication program.
Set up a Commercial Real Estate Lending User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңCommercial Real Estate LendingГўВҖВқ. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Add Commercial Real Estate Lending
Select
OK
to confirm.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Related Information
Commercial Real Estate Lending Guide,
Form 9412
8.8	Corporate Capture
Corporate Capture allows your business customers to securely scan Canadian and US dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders and transmit them to Central 1 for processing. Your financial institution can elect to have business customers scan and transmit Corporate Capture deposits directly to Central 1 for processing, or your staff can review deposits and transmit them to Central 1 on your business customersГўВҖВҷ behalf. You decide which workflow or combination of workflows to use for your business customers.
The Corporate Capture workflow(s) that your financial institution selects for processing Corporate Capture deposits, as well as the responsibilities granted to users, affects how you set up your business customers. The following table provides four workflow options and the roles to assign users. Before setting up users, review the Corporate Capture workflows and user type descriptions in the Deposit Processing Part for more information.
Note:	Businesses can have a mix of separated and combined user types as appropriate.
Workflow Option
User Types to Assign Users
Separated User Types (individuals performing separate tasks) ГўВҖВ“ Financial Institution Reviews and Transmits Corporate Capture Deposits
Corporate Deposit Capture
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to business customers.
Corporate Deposit Supervisor
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to staff at your financial institution.
Corporate Deposit Analyst
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to business customers but not those with the Corporate Deposit Capture role.
Separated User Types (individuals performing separate tasks) ГўВҖВ“ Financial Institution Monitors Corporate Capture Deposits
Corporate Deposit Capture
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to business customers.
Corporate Deposit Supervisor
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to business customers but not those with the Corporate Deposit Capture role.
Corporate Deposit Analyst
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to staff at your financial institution.
Combined User Types ГўВҖВ“ Financial Institution Reviews and Transmits Corporate Capture Deposits
Corporate Deposit Capture
and
Corporate Deposit Analyst
ГўВҖВ“ Assign both user types to each business customer.
Corporate Deposit Supervisor
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to staff at your financial institution.
Combined User Types ГўВҖВ“ Financial Institution Monitors Corporate Capture Deposits
Corporate Deposit Capture
and
Corporate Deposit Supervisor
ГўВҖВ“ Assign both user types to each business customer.
Corporate Deposit Analyst
ГўВҖВ“ Assign to staff at your financial institution.
Set up a Corporate Capture User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңCorporate CaptureГўВҖВқ. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Add Corporate Capture
Complete the Corporate Capture permissions as follows. See Figure 12.
ГўВ‘
Add Transit Number
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the business clientГўВҖВҷs unlisted transit number.
ГўВҲВ’
To add multiple transit numbers to a user (if applicable), enter a transit number, then select the
Enter
key on your keyboard to create a new line. Repeat until youГўВҖВҷve entered all transit numbers. You can assign a maximum of 44 transits.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Add
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select User Type
ГўВҖВ“ Select the check box(es) next to the userГўВҖВҷs authorized user type(s). Multiple user types can be assigned to a user. See the Corporate Capture workflows and user type information in the Deposit Processing Part for more information. Refer to the table above for an explanation of what role(s) to assign to business customers and what role to assign to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs staff based on your selected workflow. Choose from:
ГўВҲВ’
Corporate Deposit Capture
ГўВҖВ“ Create and balance deposits. Create deposit reports for owned deposits. This role is always assigned to the business customer.
ГўВҲВ’
Corporate Deposit Supervisor
ГўВҖВ“ Review and transmit deposits. Create deposit reports for all users at the assigned transit(s). This user type may be assigned to the business customer or to staff at your financial institution depending on the workflow selected.
ГўВҲВ’
Corporate Deposit Analyst
ГўВҖВ“ View deposits and create reports for assigned transit(s). This user type may be assigned to the business customer or to staff at your financial institution depending on the workflow selected.
Note: 	There isnГўВҖВҷt a Corporate Capture Administrator user type because Branch Capture Administrators automatically have access to all your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs locations. B
usiness customers enrolled in Corporate Capture essentially become an extension of your financial institution. As a result, Branch Capture Administrators can access customer locations when generating reports
.
ГўВ‘Вў
Indicate if the User is Staff or a Corporate Client
ГўВҲВ’
Check the ГўВҖВңIs a Corporate ClientГўВҖВқ box if the user is a business customer. This prevents the user from accessing Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Client Centre.
Important
:	DonГўВҖВҷt check the ГўВҖВңIs a Corporate ClientГўВҖВқ box for internal staff or they wonГўВҖВҷt have access to the Client Centre.
Select
Submit
.
Figure 12: Complete Corporate Capture Permissions
Review the summary of changes, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Note:	This application doesnГўВҖВҷt require approval from another Security Officer.
8.9	Corporate Client Files and Reports
Corporate Client Files and Reports allow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs business customers to access the File and Report Exchange. Business customers must be set up as Client Centre Users as theyГўВҖВҷre authenticated on the Client Centre; however, they access File and Report Exchange using a different URL than financial institution users. Along with granting access to Corporate Client Files and Reports in User Management, you must also complete FTP User Request, Form 2010. Since additional set up is required at Central 1, business customers should wait for confirmation before attempting to log in to the File and Report Exchange. See the File and Report Distribution Part for more information.
Set up a Corporate Client Files and Reports User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңCorporate Client Files and ReportsГўВҖВқ. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Add Corporate Clients Files and Reports
Select
OK
to confirm.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Related Information
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
8.10	Current Account System (CAS) Online
Current Account System (CAS) Online allows authorized users at your financial institution to view its Central 1 account information in near real-time. Account information is available for each currency type your financial institution maintains with Central 1 (i.e., Canadian dollars, US dollars, Pound Sterling, and Euros).
You canГўВҖВҷt assign users access to CAS Online. Central 1 manages all CAS Online users. However, if you want the user to access CAS Online via the Client Centre (rather than a direct URL), create a Client Centre ID for the user as described in Section 6.2, Creating a Client Centre User if they donГўВҖВҷt already have one. Central 1 will assign the individual access to CAS Online upon receipt of the CAS Online User Request, Form 2513. See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part procedures. The link on the Client Centre goes to the same URL as the direct URL and requires the user to enter their separate CAS username and password.
Important
:	Ensure that the Client Centre ID isnГўВҖВҷt the same as the individualГўВҖВҷs CAS Online ID.
Related Information
CAS Online User Request,
Form 2513
8.11	EFM (Enterprise Fraud Management)
Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) allows authorized users at your financial institution to view alerts for potentially fraudulent Interac eГўВҖВ‘Transfer
ГӮВ®
and Wires transactions. Users review alerts generated on EFM to investigate suspected fraud and report the results on EFM. See the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
Four user roles are available in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM): Tenant Reports, Junior Investigator, Senior Investigator, and Team Administrator. The following table describes the permissions for each role. You can only assign one role to an EFM User.
Permission
Definition
Tenant Reports
Junior Investigator
Senior Investigator
Team
Administrator
Add to AMDL List
Enables the ГўВҖВңAdd to ListГўВҖВқ option during incident review. This adds data from the current entity's event history to a data list.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Audit Log
Enables viewing of the audit log of tenant user interactions with the tenant UI.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Bulk Delete Incidents
Enables multiple incidents to be deleted without reviewing simultaneously.
ГҜВғВј
Bulk Filter Incidents
Allows filtering of incidents (in the Incident List) based on criteria that includes time, risk score, triggered rules, and models.
ГҜВғВј
Bulk Refer Incidents
Enables multiple incidents to be referred to a tenant user or team simultaneously.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Bulk Review Incidents
Enables all alerts in multiple incidents to be classified as ГўВҖВңRiskГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңNo-riskГўВҖВқ simultaneously.
ГҜВғВј
Data Lists
Enables the creation and management of data lists at the tenant level. This also allows the direct addition or deletion of values in data lists and import of data into data lists in CSV format.
ГҜВғВј
Documentation
Enables the inline documentation sidebar.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Event Search Box
Enables the entity search box (in the EFM header), as well as access to the ГўВҖВңEventsГўВҖВқ UI page.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Incident Commenter
Enables incident commenting functionality.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Incident Filters
Enables the creation and editing of incident list filters based on triggered rules, tags, and/or team referred to.
ГҜВғВј
Incident Manual Alert
Enables the creation of manual alerts on an incident
ГҜВғВј
Incident Referrer
Allows the user to refer incidents to other teams or users.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Incident Referrer Viewable
Allows the user to view incidents that have been referred to other teams or users.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Incident Reviewer
Enables incident review functionality.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Incidents
Enables viewing the incident list in the Solution or tenant UI and read-only access to incident detail pages (to review incidents, a user must have the ГўВҖВңIncident ReviewerГўВҖВқ permission).
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Message Broadcast
Enables user to send messages to other users within the same tenant.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Pending Incidents
Allows the user to view the ГўВҖВңPendingГўВҖВқ list of incidents and add incidents to this list.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Teams
Enables the creation of teams and assignment of users to teams.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Unlock Incidents
Allows the unlocking of incidents being viewed by another user.
ГҜВғВј
View All Incidents
Enables selection of ГўВҖВңAll IncidentsГўВҖВқ in the Incident List filter drop-down menu. Without this permission, a user can only view the Incident List using incident filters.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Management Information
Access management information categories in the Reports section.
ГҜВғВј
ГҜВғВј
Set up an EFM User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңFraud Alert ServicesГўВҖВқ. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Add Fraud Alert Services
Complete the EFM permissions as follows. See Figure 15.
Select the check box next to the role to which the user requires access. Choose from the following options. Assign only one role. See the table above for role descriptions.
ГўВҲВ’
CU Senior Investigator
ГўВҲВ’
CU Junior Investigator
ГўВҲВ’
CU Team Admin
ГўВҲВ’
CU Tenant Report
Select
Submit
.
Figure 15: Complete EFM Permissions
Review the summary of changes, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Note: 	EFM Users must have a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre profile to access EFM. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens, for procedures.
8.12	File and Report Exchange
File and Report Exchange allows authorized users at your financial institution to upload and download files (e.g., AFT, clearing and deposit items, bill payments) and to access Central 1 account activity and statements. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information.
Note:	Both financial institution and business customers can connect to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange. For procedures on managing business customer access, see Section 8.9, Corporate Client Files and Reports.
Set up a File and Report Exchange User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңFile and Report ExchangeГўВҖВқ. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Add File and Report Exchange
Select the check box(es) next to the appropriate File and Report Exchange permission(s), then select
Submit
. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Complete File and Report Folder Permissions
Review the summary of changes, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
8.13	Forge OpenText ГўВ„Вў Experience Studio
Forge OpenText Experience Studio (Experience Studio) is the content management system that  authorized users at your financial institution use to manage your Forge public website. See Forge OpenText Creating & Updating Pages and Forge OpenText Component Library for more information.
Set up a Forge Experience OpenText Studio User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңTeamsiteГўВҖВқ. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Add Teamsite
Select
OK
to confirm.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Related Information
Forge OpenText Creating & Updating Pages
Forge OpenText Component Library
8.14	Forge OpenTextГўВ„Вў CC Professional
Forge OpenText CC Professional is the content management system that authorized users at your financial institution use to manage certain aspects of your Forge Online Banking website and Forge Mobile App. This application is known as TeamSite to
MemberDirect
ГӮВ®
users. See the OpenText CC Professional Part in the Forge Manual for more information.
Note: 	Forge OpenText CC Professional Users must have a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre profile to access Forge OpenText CC Professional. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens, for procedures.
Set up a Forge OpenText CC Professional User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңTeamsiteГўВҖВқ. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Add Teamsite
Select
OK
to confirm.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Note: 	Forge OpenText Experience Studio Users must have a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre profile to access Forge OpenText Experience Studio. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens, for procedures.
8.15	Fraud Alert System (FAS)
Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Fraud Alert System (FAS) allows authorized users at your financial institutions to securely report and share information about debit card skimming originating from a Common Point of Purchase (CPP), providing critical information required to mitigate losses and make informed risk management decisions. FAS is also used for Interac e-Transfer payments that arenГўВҖВҷt captured in EFM (e.g., payments from financial institutions that arenГўВҖВҷt on
MemberDirect
with Enhanced User Features (EUF) or 3.4 API).
Set up a Fraud Alert System User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңFraud Alert System (FAS)ГўВҖВқ. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Add Fraud Alert System (FAS)
Select
OK
to confirm.
Note:	This application doesnГўВҖВҷt require approval from another Security Officer.
8.16	FX Drafts
FX Drafts allows authorized users at your financial institution to create and print drafts in-branch. Drafts are available in more than 20 foreign currencies, including US dollars. These drafts are negotiable instruments drawn on ConveraГўВҖВҷs accounts with its international partner banks.
There are two user management components for FX Drafts Users. First, users must be set up as a Client Centre User or Security Officer with access to FX Drafts. Second, an FX Drafts user with admin access must set up permissions for the user within the FX Drafts application. This section describes how to assign access to FX Drafts via User Management. See the FX Drafts Part for procedures on managing users in the FX Drafts application.
Set up an FX Drafts User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңFX DraftsГўВҖВқ. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Add FX Drafts
Select
OK
to confirm.

8.19	Increased Authentication Admin
Increased Authentication is a security feature that strengthens online and mobile banking security by using stepped-up authentication during high-risk online banking activities. Increased Authentication for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs digital banking solutions uses OutseerГўВ„Вў Fraud Manager security software from RSA
ГӮВ®
Security LLC to assess user online banking activities and identify high-risk situations where stronger authentication is required. See the Increased Authentication Administration Quick Start Guide for more information. Users can also be granted access to Case Manager, a component of Outseer Fraud Manager, that allows authorized users at your financial institution to view user activities and their authentication history, as well as increased authentication reports. See the Risk Engine & Case Manager Quick Start Guide for more information.

or

8.22	Money Transfer System (MTS)
MTS Administrators at your financial institution use the Money Transfer System (MTS) to manage Wires Users, other MTS Administrators, and branch settings for the Wires application. They also authorize changes in MTS for other MTS Administrators. Only MTS Administrators have access to MTS.
MTS Administrators are set up on two systems. First, the individual must be a Client Centre User or  Security Officer with access to MTS. This section describes how to assign MTS access via User Management. Complete this step first because the individualГўВҖВҷs Client Centre username is required in MTS. Second, set up the MTS Administrator on MTS. See the Wires Part for procedures.
Note: 	Central 1 creates your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs first two MTS Administrators. You can then add, edit, and delete MTS Administrators and Wires Users as required.
The Wires application is used to send and receive wires and is only available to Wires Users. Although MTS Administrators create Wires Users on MTS and assign them permissions and limits, Wires Users canГўВҖВҷt access MTS. See Section 8.44, Wires, for procedures on assigning access to Wires.
Set up an MTS Administrator as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңMTS (Money Transfer System)ГўВҖВқ. See Figure 29.
Figure 29: Add MTS (Money Transfer System)
Enter the first few digits of the transit number in the Transit Number field on the MTS (Money Transfer System) permissions, then select the appropriate transit number from the list. Select
Add
, then select
Submit
. See Figure 30. Alternatively, enter an unlisted transit number.
Note: 	The transit number that you enter here is considered the MTS AdministratorГўВҖВҷs primary or home transit number on MTS. It must be the same transit number that you use for their MTS profile.
Figure 30: Complete MTS (Money Transfer System) Permissions

Confirm
. Alternatively, select

or

as required.

8.24	Online Return System (ORS)
The Online Return System (ORS) allows authorized users at your financial institution to return cheques and AFT transactions. ORS also allows users to view incoming clearing items (including chargebacks), research deposit items (including deposit adjustments), reinstate cheques, and create AFT Notice of Change transactions. Your financial institution can elect to upload your cheque return files, AFT return files, and AFT Notice of Change files to ORS manually or via an automated script. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
The Security Officer determines the username and user type for each ORS user.
There are four ORS user types: Research Users, Return Users, Upload Users, and Script Users, as described below.
User Type
Permissions
Research User
ГўВҖВў
Search, view, print, and save images of cleared cheques (including suspected duplicates)
ГўВҖВў
Search, view, print, and save adjustments, deposit slips, and cheques
Return User
ГўВҖВў
All the permissions of Research Users
ГўВҖВў
Search, view, print, and save AFT transactions
ГўВҖВў
Return AFT transactions
ГўВҖВў
Create AFT Notice of Change transactions
ГўВҖВў
Return dishonoured cheques
ГўВҖВў
Reinstate incorrect information on cheques, including transit number, account number, and serial (cheque) number
ГўВҖВў
View and print daily ORS reports that summarize cheque and AFT returns entered in ORS
Upload Users
ГўВҖВў
All the permissions of a Return User
ГўВҖВў
Manually upload Canadian Cheque Return files (.RI), USD Cheque Return files (.RIU), AFT Return files (.RTN), and AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) to ORS
Script Users
ГўВҖВў
Upload Canadian Cheque Return files (.RI), USD Cheque Return files (.RIU), AFT Return Files (.RTN), and AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) manually to ORS or via an automated script

Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңOnline Return SystemГўВҖВқ. See Figure 33.
Figure 33: Add Online Return System
Complete the ORS permissions as follows. See Figure 34.
ГўВ‘
User Name
ГўВҖВ“ Enter a username (maximum eight characters). This name is used to log actions in ORS. The username doesnГўВҖВҷt have to match the userГўВҖВҷs Client Centre ID.
Note: 	DonГўВҖВҷt include spaces or special characters in the username.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
User Type
ГўВҖВ“ Enter one of the following user types: Research, Return, Upload, or Script, as described below. This field isnГўВҖВҷt case-sensitive.
ГўВҲВ’
Research
ГўВҖВ“
Search, view, print, and save images of cleared cheques (including suspected duplicates), adjustments, deposit slips, and cheques on deposit.
ГўВҲВ’
Return
ГўВҖВ“
All the permissions of Research users, plus search, view, print, and save AFT transactions, return AFT transactions, create AFT Notice of Change transactions, return dishonoured cheques, reinstate incorrect information on cheques, including the transit number, account number, and serial (cheque) number, and view and print daily ORS reports that summarize cheque and AFT returns entered in ORS.
Note:	Only AFT-activated financial institutions can view, print, save, and return AFT transactions.
ГўВҲВ’
Upload ГўВҖВ“
All the permissions of Returns users, plus, manually upload Canadian cheque return files (.RI), USD cheque return files (.RIU), AFT return files (.RTN), and AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) to ORS.
ГўВҲВ’
Script ГўВҖВ“
Upload Canadian cheque return files (.RI), USD cheque return files (.RIU), AFT return files (.RTN), and AFT Notice of Change files (.NOC) manually to ORS or via an automated script.
Select
Submit
.

. Alternatively, select

or

as required.

8.38
PaymentStream
Direct Test Environments
PaymentStream
Direct is a user interface that provides access to payments products, digital banking support features, and related administration functions via a single sign-on to the Client Centre. The options you see on
PaymentStream
Direct are determined by the functions that your financial institution implements, and a Client Centre User or Security OfficerГўВҖВҷs personal access rights.
PaymentStream
Direct functions are listed as individual applications in the User Management application. See the application specific information for a description of each function. Client Centre Users and Security Officers only see the functions to which theyГўВҖВҷre authorized. See the
PaymentStream
ГўВ„Вў Direct Part for more information.
PaymentStream
Direct test environments are also listed as individual applications. Security Officers with administrative rights to the PSD test environments can assign them to Client Centre Users and other Security Officers. Users that require access must be assigned the appropriate test environment(s) and the appropriate application(s).
Important
:	Users granted access to test environments are also granted access to production. If you donГўВҖВҷt want a testing user to access production, you must assign the ГўВҖВңPSD-DenyГўВҖВқ application to the Client Centre User or Security Officer.
The following applications allow access to the available
PaymentStream
Direct test environments:
Application ID
Description
PaymentStream
Direct Deny
Prevents a Client Centre User or Security Officer from accessing the
PaymentStream
Direct production environment when assigned to a test environment.
PaymentStream
Direct PrevCompProd
Allows a Client Centre User or Security Officer access to the PrevCompProd test environment. Users must also be assigned the appropriate
PaymentStream
Direct application(s) e.g., Wires, Bill Payments.
Note: Users can access to both the test and production environments unless theyГўВҖВҷre assigned the ГўВҖВңPSD-DenyГўВҖВқ application.
PaymentStream
Direct PrevCompTest
Allows a Client Centre User or Security Officer access to the PrevCompTest test environment.
Users must also be assigned the appropriate
PaymentStream
Direct application(s) e.g., Wires, Bill Payments.
Note: Users can access to both the test and production environments unless theyГўВҖВҷre assigned the ГўВҖВңPSD-DenyГўВҖВқ application.
PaymentStream
Direct SYS
Allows a Client Centre User or Security Officer access to the PSD-SYS test environment.
Users must also be assigned the appropriate
PaymentStream
Direct application(s) e.g., Wires, Bill Payments.
Note: Users can access to both the test and production environments unless theyГўВҖВҷre assigned the ГўВҖВңPSD-DenyГўВҖВқ application.
PaymentStream
Direct SYSProd
Allows a Client Centre User or Security Officer access to the SYSProd test environment.
Users must also be assigned the appropriate
PaymentStream
Direct application(s) e.g., Wires, Bill Payments.
Note: Users can access to both the test and production environments unless theyГўВҖВҷre assigned the ГўВҖВңPSD-DenyГўВҖВқ application.

Direct. See the

Direct Part for more information.

8.41	Treasury Connect
Treasury Connect is an online portal that allows authorized users at your financial institution to book deposits and loans, view Index-Linked Term Deposits (ILTD) sales history, enter ILTD sales totals, transfer funds between your Central 1 accounts, and view your Central 1 account activity and rates. Treasury Connect also provides access to other Treasury services like FX Trader and provides links to information that Treasury teams will find useful, such as Market Rates, Treasury Reports, and Market Expertise. See the Treasury Connect User Guide, Form 6444, for more information.
Set up a Treasury Connect User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add

.

.

8.42	Treasury Reports
Treasury Reports allows authorized users at your financial institutions to view and download reports and statements for Central 1 Treasury transactions (e.g., deposits, FX, loans, derivatives). See the Treasury Reports User Guide, Form 6158, for more information. Users access Treasury Reports via Treasury Connect.
Note:	To access Treasury Reports via Treasury Connect, users must also be assigned Treasury Connect. See Section 8.41, Treasury Connect, for procedures. See the Treasury Connect User Guide, Form 6444, for more information on Treasury Connect.

8.44	Wires
The Wires application allows authorized users at your financial institution to send and receive domestic and international wires. See the Wires Part for more information. Wires users are set up on two systems. First, the individual must be a Client Centre User or Security Officer assigned the Wires application. Complete this step first because the individualГўВҖВҷs Client Centre User ID is required to set up the userГўВҖВҷs Wires permissions in MTS. Second, an MTS Administrator sets up the Wires User in MTS and assigns their Wires application permissions. See the Wires Part for procedures on creating and managing Wires Users in MTS.
Set up a Wires User as follows:
Open the userГўВҖВҷs profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
next to ГўВҖВңWiresГўВҖВқ. See Figure 61.
Figure 61: Add Wires
Select
OK
to confirm.
Have another Security Officer authorize the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Note:	An MTS Administrator must also set up and manage the userГўВҖВҷs Wires application permissions in MTS. See the Wires Part for procedures. Only MTS Administrators are granted access to MTS. DonГўВҖВҷt grant MTS access to Wires Users.
Note: 	Wires Users who accept incoming wires and/or approve outgoing wires must also have a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre profile. See the Wires Part for more information. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens, for procedures.
